steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion: "temperature reductions were observed in the air immediately surrounding the mushrooms." Trace how this sentence connects to what precedes it and what follows it in the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'It establishes a finding central to the text's discussion of a practical application resulting from the team's study.': The sentence immediately after says the reductions "inspired an air-cooling device" — a concrete practical application. The underlined observation is the direct cause of that application, making it central to the text's point, not peripheral. ✓
  • Choice B — 'It provides empirical evidence to bolster the claim made earlier...that certain fungal species maintain a hypothermic state.': The earlier claim is that mushrooms achieve a hypothermic state through evaporative cooling. The underlined sentence describes air temperature around the mushrooms, a secondary downstream effect. It does not directly support the hypothermic-state claim about the fungi themselves. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It presents a tangential finding...that the experiment described later in the text was designed to explain.': The finding is not tangential — it directly triggered the air-cooling device. And no later experiment is designed to explain the temperature reductions; the device prototype is already described. ✗
  • Choice D — 'It identifies an unexpected observation that motivated the study of evaporative cooling in fungi that is discussed earlier.': Reverses the causal order. The study of evaporative cooling in fungi comes first in the passage; the underlined sentence is a result of that study, not what motivated it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
